opportunities.event_name: US Non-Farm Payrolls & Unemployment Rate — March 2026
opportunities.rationale: February NFP printed a soft 92K, sharply below expectations. March data confirms whether labour market is genuinely weakening under oil-driven stagflation or if Feb was a seasonal anomaly. S&P now below 200-day MA (~6,620), closing ~6,446 on Mar 31. A weak print (<100K) accelerates recession pricing toward 6,100-6,200; a strong rebound (>200K) eases growth fears but keeps Fed on hold. VIX ~29 ensures amplified reaction. Invalidation: Iran ceasefire materially shifts risk appetite before release.

opportunities.event_name: OPEC+ Joint Ministerial Monitoring Committee Meeting
opportunities.trade_setup: volatility spike — direction dependent on output decision
opportunities.rationale: Strait of Hormuz disruption and Iran conflict keep the oil risk premium elevated into the April OPEC+ meeting. A decision to maintain or deepen cuts would be bullish for crude and energy equities, while a surprise output increase would be bearish oil and supportive for broader equities. The binary nature argues for waiting for the announcement before trading. Note: USOIL directional long removed from this file after stop $89.50 was breached on Mar 25 (intraday low $86.72). Invalidation: Hormuz reopens before meeting.

ISM prints same day as OPEC+ meeting (Apr 1 double-catalyst). Sub-50 contraction confirms stagflation narrative and accelerates equity selling from S&P already below 200-day MA. Expansion above 50 with tame prices-paid would be rare bullish combo. VIX ~29 implies outsized moves. Invalidation: OPEC+ headlines dominate and ISM reaction is negligible.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long ONLY after gold holds above $4,500 for 2 consecutive daily closes AND RSI(14) daily turns up from sub-30. Scale: 50% initial on confirmation, add 50% on reclaim of $4,600.
opportunities.rationale: Gold crashed from ~$5,400 to mid-$4,400s on USD strength and hawkish FOMC repricing, then stabilised with XAU/USD quoted around $4,558 on Mar 31. Stop at $4,180 has not been hit (low since crash was ~$4,448). R:R from $4,435 midpoint to $4,850 target vs $4,180 stop ~1:1.6. Iran war safe-haven bid and stagflation backdrop remain intact. Invalidation: daily close below $4,180 or Fed signals 0 cuts for 2026.

opportunities.trade_setup: safe-haven USD continuation into event cluster
opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter short at 1.1550-1.1650 into the early-April macro cluster (OPEC+, ISM, NFP). Add on hot US data or weak Eurozone prints. Exit on dovish Fed repricing or confirmed Iran de-escalation.
opportunities.rationale: EUR/USD closed ~1.1480 on Mar 31 after oscillating mid-1.14s to mid-1.16s since late March. Stop at 1.1750 not hit (max high since Mar 22 was ~1.1640). QG3 pass confirmed. Fed cautious cutting path vs ECB under pressure to ease keeps rate differential USD-positive. R:R from 1.1600 midpoint to 1.1300 vs 1.1750 ~1:2.0. Invalidation: daily close above 1.1750.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter short on any bounce into 6,550-6,650 zone (prior 200-day MA support, now resistance). Requires daily close below 200-day MA. Skip if VIX drops below 22 or Iran ceasefire announced.
opportunities.rationale: S&P closed ~6,446 on Mar 31 after failing to reclaim 20-, 50-, and 200-day MAs. Stop at 6,750 not hit (max high since Mar 22 was 6,651.62 on Mar 23). QG3 pass confirmed. TP at 6,150 not hit (low was 6,316.91 on Mar 30). QG4 pass. R:R from 6,600 midpoint to 6,150 vs 6,750 ~1:3. Invalidation: daily close above 6,750.
